memory_entries.text: In this SQLite product database, office products are group g1: items_g1 joined to fdbk_g1 on ref_id. items_g1 has price column prc (REAL), main_cat contains mostly 'Office Products' but the dataset should be treated as office overall unless the question explicitly asks for main_cat filtering. fdbk_g1.ts is mostly 13-digit Unix milliseconds; use ms epoch ranges such as 2022-01-01=1640995200000 and 2023-01-01=1672531200000.

In this SQLite product database, electronics products are group g2: items_g2 joined to fdbk_g2 on ref_id. items_g2 has price columns prc (INTEGER) and prc_usd (REAL); use prc_usd for dollar price filters. fdbk_g2 has review rating rtg and timestamp ts.
In the electronics items_g2 table, the 'Computers & Accessories' top-level category appears as main_cat='Computers' (not the full label).

In this SQLite product database, musical instruments are group g3: items_g3 joined to fdbk_g3 on ref_id. fdbk_g3.ts is stored as ISO date text (YYYY-MM-DD), so use substr(ts,1,7) to group by month; the busiest musical-instrument review month found was 2020-01 with 4715 reviews.
Office products without any attributes but with at least one review: count distinct items_g1.ref_id joined to fdbk_g1 and anti-joined to attrs_g1; result was 267.

In the musical instruments group g3, items_g3.str_nm is empty for brand; product brand is stored in attrs_g3 with attr_key='Brand'. Counting DISTINCT ref_id by attr_val shows the most common musical-instrument brand is Fender (243 products).
In the electronics fdbk_g2 table, ts may be mixed Unix seconds and milliseconds: Q4 2021 reviews require seconds epoch range 1633046400 to 1640995200, which counted 1867 reviews.
Electronics products with zero reviews: 11,345 distinct items in items_g2 with no fdbk_g2 row; 3,253 have prc_usd non-NULL and non-zero, so the price-listed percentage is 28.67%.

Correction from prior episode: electronics products with zero reviews and price-listed percentage should be 29.93%, not 28.67%; likely denominator/numerator definition or price condition differs from my earlier memory.

Across the two product groups with attribute tables (office g1 attrs_g1 and musical instruments g3 attrs_g3), products listed with attr_key='Is Discontinued By Manufacturer', attr_val='Yes', and catalog avg_rtg < 3.0 count as: g1=17, g3=4, total=21.
Office products listing date is stored in attrs_g1 attr_key='Date First Available'; counting products with year before 2015 by substr(trim(attr_val),-4) yielded 5254.
Electronics items_g2 has catalog image count in img_ct; median prc_usd for electronics with img_ct>=1 and prc_usd>0 was 20.95 (n=6344) in Question 21.
Correction from Question 21 feedback: median prc_usd for electronics with catalog image count condition was 22.09, not 20.95; my earlier memory mem_04249efd is stale/incorrect.
Electronics products showing at least 3 catalog images and positive list price: AVG(prc_usd) rounded to 2 decimals is 52.94 (n=4886).

Correction from Question 25 feedback: electronics products showing at least 3 catalog images and positive list price should have average/answer 101.8, not 52.94; prior memory mem_72c19d2e is stale or used wrong image-count interpretation.

Musical instruments attribute current-vs-legacy tables: product_attributes_g3 is the current feed and attrs_g3_legacy is the older attribute export; row counts 202,543 vs 182,566, ratio current/legacy rounded to 2 decimals = 1.11.

Musical instruments current-vs-legacy brand coverage: distinct products with nonblank current product_attributes_g3 attr_key='Brand' but no attrs_g3_legacy row with attr_key='Brand' = 1145. Efficient query used EXCEPT over DISTINCT ref_id sets; NOT EXISTS timed out.

Musical instruments reviews fdbk_g3 has populated review_year/review_month columns for almost all reviews (286,623/286,626) while ts is populated for fewer (200,636); for calendar-year review questions use fdbk_g3.review_year. Instrument brands are in product_attributes_g3 attr_key='Brand'. Brands with at least one review in each of 2019, 2020, and 2021 = 891.
